ncert_truth_statements: The graph of current (I) against voltage (V) for an ohmic conductor is a straight line through the origin, confirming I is directly proportional to V
The resistance R equals V divided by I; on a V-I graph the slope of the line equals 1/R — a steeper slope means lower resistance
Doubling the applied voltage doubles the current through the resistor — the I-V relationship is linear and passes through the origin
A higher resistance produces a shallower slope on the I-V graph, meaning less current flows for the same voltage
The current through a conductor is proportional to the potential difference applied across its ends, provided temperature and physical conditions remain constant
